{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "QA Automation Developer",
  "description": "At Strivo Labs, we are passionate about helping businesses scale with speed and precision. We specialise in providing end-to-end digital solutions, from custom design and MVP development to automation and content creation.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: QA Automation Developer

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos

Role Summary


	We are seeking a detail-oriented QA Automation Developer to ensure the quality, reliability, and performance of our web applications, mobile applications, automation workflows, and WhatsApp-based solutions.


Key Responsibilities


	Testing &amp; Execution: Perform manual and automated testing for web and mobile applications. Conduct regression, functional, performance, and UAT (User Acceptance Testing) testing.
	Automation &amp; Workflow Validation: Validate n8n workflows, automations, and integrations. Test WhatsApp chatbot flows, messaging automation, and user journeys.
	API &amp; Integration Testing: Conduct API testing and integration testing.
	Defect Management: Identify, document, track, and verify software defects.
	Collaboration &amp; Standards: Collaborate closely with developers, product managers, and stakeholders. Establish QA standards and testing best practices to ensure releases meet quality benchmarks before deployment.
	Strategy: Design and execute comprehensive test plans and test cases.


Requirements


	Experience: 3+ years of QA experience.
	Application Testing: Experience testing web and mobile applications.
	Specialized Systems: Strong knowledge of n8n automation workflows and experience testing WhatsApp Business Platform solutions.
	Tools &amp; Frameworks: API testing experience using Postman or similar tools alongside experience with automated testing frameworks.
	Methodology: Knowledge of Agile methodologies.
	Core Skills: Excellent documentation and communication skills.


Preferred Qualifications:


	Experience with CI/CD pipelines.
	Experience with Selenium, Cypress, Playwright, or similar tools.
	Security and performance testing experience.
	ISTQB Certification is an advantage.
",
  "datePosted": "2026-06-19",
  "validThrough": "2026-07-20",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",
  "hiringOrganization": {
    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "Strivo Labs"
    
  },

    "@type": "Place",
    "address": {
      "@type": "PostalAddress",
       "postalCode": ". ",
       "addressRegion": ". ",
       "streetAddress": ". ",
      "addressLocality": "Lagos",
      "addressCountry": "NG"
     
      
    }
  },
  "identifier": {
    "@type": "PropertyValue",
    "name": "",
    "value": "200675"
  },
  "baseSalary": {
    "@type": "MonetaryAmount",
    "currency": "NGN",
    "value": {
      "@type": "QuantitativeValue",
      "minValue": 0,
      "maxValue": 0,
      "unitText": "MONTH"
    }

}



QA Automation Developer Job at Strivo Labs




        
    

        
        At Strivo Labs, we are passionate about helping businesses scale with speed and precision. We specialise in providing end-to-end digital solutions, from custom design and MVP development to automation and content creation.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: QA Automation Developer

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos

Role Summary


	We are seeking a detail-oriented QA Automation Developer to ensure the quality, reliability, and performance of our web applications, mobile applications, automation workflows, and WhatsApp-based solutions.


Key Responsibilities
     

            
    




	Testing & Execution: Perform manual and automated testing for web and mobile applications. Conduct regression, functional, performance, and UAT (User Acceptance Testing) testing.
	Automation & Workflow Validation: Validate n8n workflows, automations, and integrations. Test WhatsApp chatbot flows, messaging automation, and user journeys.
	API & Integration Testing: Conduct API testing and integration testing.
	Defect Management: Identify, document, track, and verify software defects.
	Collaboration & Standards: Collaborate closely with developers, product managers, and stakeholders. Establish QA standards and testing best practices to ensure releases meet quality benchmarks before deployment.
	Strategy: Design and execute comprehensive test plans and test cases.


Requirements


	Experience: 3+ years of QA experience.
	Application Testing: Experience testing web and mobile applications.
	Specialized Systems: Strong knowledge of n8n automation workflows and experience testing WhatsApp Business Platform solutions.
	Tools & Frameworks: API testing experience using Postman or similar tools alongside experience with automated testing frameworks.
	Methodology: Knowledge of Agile methodologies.
	Core Skills: Excellent documentation and communication skills.


Preferred Qualifications:


	Experience with CI/CD pipelines.
	Experience with Selenium, Cypress, Playwright, or similar tools.
	Security and performance testing experience.
	ISTQB Certification is an advantage.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Customer Relations Officer",
  "description": "Zinajob is an employment and recruitment platform operating in Nigeria, serving as a hub connecting job seekers with open career opportunities across the country.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Customer Relations Officer

Job Location: Lekki, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilties


	Support the customer service team in handling inquiries, complaints, and requests in a professional and timely manner.
	Receive and record customer orders accurately, including service type, item details, and delivery timelines.
	Tag and label clothing items correctly upon receipt to ensure proper identification and tracking throughout the cleaning process.
	Respond to basic customer calls, messages, and walk-in inquiries, and escalate complex issues to senior staff when necessary.
	Issue receipts and maintain order tags to ensure proper tracking of garments from intake to delivery.
	Maintain and update customer records, contact details, and order history using Microsoft Excel and internal registers.
	Assist in preparing simple reports on customer feedback, complaints, and service performance using Excel and Word.
	Help track and follow up on customer orders to ensure timely completion and delivery.
	Support the preparation of customer communication materials such as notices, updates, and service information using Word and PowerPoint.
	Assist in gathering and summarizing customer feedback for service improvement purposes.
	Schedule and coordinate customer pick-up and delivery follow-ups where required.
	Support the team in maintaining high levels of customer satisfaction through consistent communication and service updates.
	Assist in preparing presentation slides for internal reviews or service performance reports.


Qualifications


	Minimum of an HND or BSc Degree in any relevant field.
	Basic computer skills, including Microsoft Word, Excel, and PowerPoint.
	Good communication skills (verbal and written).
	Ability to read, write, and accurately record customer and order details.
	Strong attention to detail, especially in tagging, recording, and tracking clothing items.
	Willingness to learn and work in a fast-paced service environment.
	Prior experience in customer service or laundry operations is an added advantage but not required.


Salary
N100,000 monthly.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: info@zinajob.com using &quot;Application &ndash; Customer Relations Officer&quot; as the subject of the mail.",
  "datePosted": "2026-06-19",
  "validThrough": "2026-06-27",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",
  "hiringOrganization": {
    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "Zinajob Synergy"
    
  },
  "jobLocation": {
    "@type": "Place",
    "address": {
      "@type": "PostalAddress",
       "postalCode": ". ",
       "addressRegion": ". ",
       "streetAddress": ". ",
      "addressLocality": "Lagos",
      "addressCountry": "NG"
     
      
    }
  },
  "identifier": {
    "@type": "PropertyValue",
    "name": "",
    "value": "200674"
  },
  "baseSalary": {
    "@type": "MonetaryAmount",
    "currency": "NGN",
    "value": {
      "@type": "QuantitativeValue",
      "minValue": 75000,
      "maxValue": 150000,
      "unitText": "MONTH"
    }
  }
}



Customer Relations Officer Job at Zinajob




        
    

        
        Zinajob is an employment and recruitment platform operating in Nigeria, serving as a hub connecting job seekers with open career opportunities across the country.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Customer Relations Officer

Job Location: Lekki, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilties
     

            
    




	Support the customer service team in handling inquiries, complaints, and requests in a professional and timely manner.
	Receive and record customer orders accurately, including service type, item details, and delivery timelines.
	Tag and label clothing items correctly upon receipt to ensure proper identification and tracking throughout the cleaning process.
	Respond to basic customer calls, messages, and walk-in inquiries, and escalate complex issues to senior staff when necessary.
	Issue receipts and maintain order tags to ensure proper tracking of garments from intake to delivery.
	Maintain and update customer records, contact details, and order history using Microsoft Excel and internal registers.
	Assist in preparing simple reports on customer feedback, complaints, and service performance using Excel and Word.
	Help track and follow up on customer orders to ensure timely completion and delivery.
	Support the preparation of customer communication materials such as notices, updates, and service information using Word and PowerPoint.
	Assist in gathering and summarizing customer feedback for service improvement purposes.
	Schedule and coordinate customer pick-up and delivery follow-ups where required.
	Support the team in maintaining high levels of customer satisfaction through consistent communication and service updates.
	Assist in preparing presentation slides for internal reviews or service performance reports.


Qualifications


	Minimum of an HND or BSc Degree in any relevant field.
	Basic computer skills, including Microsoft Word, Excel, and PowerPoint.
	Good communication skills (verbal and written).
	Ability to read, write, and accurately record customer and order details.
	Strong attention to detail, especially in tagging, recording, and tracking clothing items.
	Willingness to learn and work in a fast-paced service environment.
	Prior experience in customer service or laundry operations is an added advantage but not required.


Salary
N100,000 monthly.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using "Application – Customer Relations Officer" as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

Administrative Officer Job at Amadeus University




        
    

        
        Amadeus University is a private University located in Amizi, Ikwuano LGA, Abia State. Recently approved by the Federal Government of Nigeria and licenced by the National Universities Commission (NUC), the University is a unique brand in Nigerian tertiary education, devoted to innovation, excellence, and high ethical standards, providing dynamic education and research tailored to the demands of the 21st century.

Applications are invited from suitably qualified candidates for the position below:

Job Position: Administrative Officer

Job Location: Amizi, Abia

Job Requirements
     

            
    




	Applicants must possess a honours Degree in the relevant field from a recognized University, plus at least two (2) years post qualification experience.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should submit their Application Letter with a detailed Curriculum Vitae, copies of all claimed certificates and their most recent digital headshot/passport photograph (to be combined into a single PDF file). The Curriculum Vitae should indicate, among others, the following:


	Biographical data
	Home address;
	Contact information (mobile and email);
	Educational history and qualifications;
	Publications as appropriate;
	Administrative experience & record of service in Universities
	Names and Contacts of three Referees, each of whom should be requested by the applicant to forward a report with subject: Reference Report on the Candidate (Name of candidate & Office Applied for).


Note
Submission of Referees Reports:


	Applicants should provide valid email addresses of their referees in the application form.
	Applicants are advised to ensure that their referees submit reports on or before the closing date, as only applications with complete referee reports will be considered.


Application / Recruitment Portal Instructions:


	Applicants are advised to carefully follow all instructions on the portal and ensure that submissions are completed on or before the closing date.
	Only applications submitted through the online recruitment portal will be considered.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Digital Marketing Associate",
  "description": "At Tybitx, we provide HR professional services rooted in Transition Management, empowering companies to achieve their goals through innovative strategies that bring incomparable results.

At Tybitx, we ensure that we are always ahead of the curve, discovering optimal solutions through our passion for continuous learning. Every service offered is a product of our free-thinking mindset, ensuring dynamic decision-making and alignment with the strategic vision of our clients, giving clients exactly what they need to succeed.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Digital Marketing Associate

Job Location: Lekki, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking a creative and results-oriented Digital Marketing Associate to strengthen our brand presence and drive engagement across online platforms.
	This role is ideal for individuals with a passion for digital storytelling, strong design skills, and the ability to translate marketing strategies into compelling campaigns.
	You will be responsible for creating engaging content, managing digital channels, and supporting lead generation efforts that fuel our real estate business. Key Responsibilities.


Key Responsibilities


	Design visually appealing graphics, social media posts, and marketing materials using Canva or other design tools.
	Execute digital marketing campaigns across social media, email, and web platforms to boost brand visibility.
	Manage company accounts to ensure consistent posting, audience engagement, and community growth.
	Monitor campaign performance, analyze data, and provide insights to optimize results.
	Work closely with the sales team to align marketing efforts with property listings and client acquisition goals.
	Maintain the company&rsquo;s core brand identity and messaging across all digital content.
	Stay updated on industry trends, competitor activities, and emerging digital tools.


Qualifications &amp; Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Marketing, Communications, Business, or a related field (preferred but not mandatory).
	1 - 2 years of experience in digital marketing, social media management, or related roles.
	Strong knowledge of graphic design tools (Canva proficiency is mandatory; knowledge of other design and video editing software is an advantage).
	Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
	Practical understanding of SEO, social media algorithms, and online advertising strategies.
	Ability to analyze sales data and translate insights into actionable steps.
",
  "datePosted": "2026-06-19",
  "validThrough": "2026-07-18",

Digital Marketing Associate Job at Tybitx Services International Limited




        
    

        
        At Tybitx, we provide HR professional services rooted in Transition Management, empowering companies to achieve their goals through innovative strategies that bring incomparable results.

At Tybitx, we ensure that we are always ahead of the curve, discovering optimal solutions through our passion for continuous learning. Every service offered is a product of our free-thinking mindset, ensuring dynamic decision-making and alignment with the strategic vision of our clients, giving clients exactly what they need to succeed.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Digital Marketing Associate

Job Location: Lekki, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

            
    




	We are seeking a creative and results-oriented Digital Marketing Associate to strengthen our brand presence and drive engagement across online platforms.
	This role is ideal for individuals with a passion for digital storytelling, strong design skills, and the ability to translate marketing strategies into compelling campaigns.
	You will be responsible for creating engaging content, managing digital channels, and supporting lead generation efforts that fuel our real estate business. Key Responsibilities.


Key Responsibilities


	Design visually appealing graphics, social media posts, and marketing materials using Canva or other design tools.
	Execute digital marketing campaigns across social media, email, and web platforms to boost brand visibility.
	Manage company accounts to ensure consistent posting, audience engagement, and community growth.
	Monitor campaign performance, analyze data, and provide insights to optimize results.
	Work closely with the sales team to align marketing efforts with property listings and client acquisition goals.
	Maintain the company’s core brand identity and messaging across all digital content.
	Stay updated on industry trends, competitor activities, and emerging digital tools.


Qualifications & Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree in Marketing, Communications, Business, or a related field (preferred but not mandatory).
	1 - 2 years of experience in digital marketing, social media management, or related roles.
	Strong knowledge of graphic design tools (Canva proficiency is mandatory; knowledge of other design and video editing software is an advantage).
	Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
	Practical understanding of SEO, social media algorithms, and online advertising strategies.
	Ability to analyze sales data and translate insights into actionable steps.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "HR Business Partner",
  "description": "Rainoil Limited is an integrated downstream oil and gas company. A prominent player in the Nigerian industry, the company&rsquo;s operations span across the downstream value chain i.e. Petroleum Product Storage, Haulage/Distribution and Retail Sales. Our primary products include Petrol (PMS), Diesel (AGO) and Kerosene (DPK).

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: HR Business Partner - Group

Job Location: Lagos
Job Type: Full Time
Workplace Type: Hybrid
Job function: People &amp; Culture

Position Overview


	As the HR Business Partner, you will play a critical, hands-on role in scaling Jaza&#39;s global people operations, systems, and group level team. You will be the Human Resources Business Partner for the Group (currently a growing team of over 40 people located in 8 countries), working closely with Jaza&rsquo;s Talent Lead, HR Lead, and Learning and Development Lead to nurture our talent, drive performance, coach managers, and build our culture.
	This is a foundational role in a lean team. You are a &#39;team of one&#39; for our Group employees.
	The ideal candidate thrives in ambiguity, loves building HR infrastructure from scratch, and shifts seamlessly from high-level leadership coaching to executing monthly data changes and payroll data verification.
	This position involves strategic partnership with global leadership, hands-on oversight of employee relations, and compliance across multiple jurisdictions.


Roles And Responsibilities
Business Partner:


	Act as the dedicated HR Business Partner for the Group (global) team of 40+ employees dispersed across 8 countries.
	Coach and advise Group managers on performance management, employee development, disciplinary actions, and organizational design.
	Coach leaders on management skills that scale across distributed, cross-cultural teams, ensuring a consistent Jaza leadership style.
	Lead employee relations for the Group team, including managing complex issues, grievances, investigations, and conflict resolution.
	Partner directly with the Talent Lead on Group talent acquisition efforts, ensuring a seamless candidate experience and strong onboarding outcomes.
	Collaborate with the Learning and Development Lead to ensure Group team training needs are identified and addressed.
	Manage the full annual performance management cycle for the Group team, including goal setting, reviews, and evaluations.
	Act as a culture champion and drive employee wellbeing, engagement activities, and culture action planning.&nbsp;


HR Administration:


	Maintain accurate and compliant employee records across all HR systems and payroll platforms, in line with internal policies and legal standards.
	Submit timely and accurate monthly group payroll data to the rewards team/finance for processing across all jurisdictions.
	Support and manage employee benefits administration globally, including documentation and processing.
	Compliance, Risk Management, and Vendor Management
	Manage group compliance and HR administrative processes.
	Own the strategic relationship with our Employer of Record (EOR) partners and leverage EORs and external counsel to build a compliant, unified global employee experience&nbsp;
	Liaise directly with local labor law experts in all operating countries (including Canada, China, Tanzania, Nigeria, Kenya, Uganda, and the United States) to maintain current knowledge and ensure full legal compliance.


How We&rsquo;ll Measure Success:


	Group managers report feeling highly supported and equipped to lead distributed, cross-cultural teams effectively, measured via leadership feedback loops and manager-readiness surveys.&nbsp;
	Zero compliance issues across all operating countries
	High accuracy in employee data and timely payroll submission
	Positive engagement survey results and clear action planning on feedback
	100% completion rate for the global performance review cycle


Key Working Relationships:


	Head of People and Culture
	Country HR Leads
	Group and Country Hiring Managers
	Talent Lead (group)
	Learning and Development Lead (group)


Why You Should Apply:


	Be part of one of the most exciting growth phases at Jaza. We&rsquo;ve proven our model, now we&rsquo;re scaling fast, and your work will directly shape our teams, culture, and long-term impact, giving you a front-row seat to meaningful systems-building and rapid organizational growth.
	Work with an international team across Canada, China, Tanzania, Nigeria, Kenya, Uganda, and the United States, gaining exposure to diverse markets, leadership styles, and cross-cultural collaboration that will accelerate your professional growth.


Qualifications / Experience &amp; Competencies


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Human Resource Management, Law, Business Administration, Psychology, or a related field.
	5 to 8 years of agile HR experience, with at least 3 years actively acting as an HR Business Partner or People Partner supporting teams across multiple African countries.
	Expertise in managing employee relations and deep, practical understanding of labor laws and practices across multiple African countries (required); experience in Asian/Western markets (Canada, China, US) is a strong plus.
	Comfortable managing relationships with Employer of Record (EOR) companies and external legal counsel.
	Detail-oriented and organized, with a strong ability to manage confidentiality and sensitive information.
	Strong communication, relationship-building, and conflict resolution skills, with a high degree of cross-cultural fluency and professionalism.
	Self-motivated and adaptable, comfortable working in a fast-paced, startup environment.

HR Business Partner Job at Rainoil Limited




        
    

        
        Rainoil Limited is an integrated downstream oil and gas company. A prominent player in the Nigerian industry, the company’s operations span across the downstream value chain i.e. Petroleum Product Storage, Haulage/Distribution and Retail Sales. Our primary products include Petrol (PMS), Diesel (AGO) and Kerosene (DPK).

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: HR Business Partner - Group

Job Location: Lagos
Job Type: Full Time
Workplace Type: Hybrid
Job function: People & Culture

Position Overview
     

            
    




	As the HR Business Partner, you will play a critical, hands-on role in scaling Jaza's global people operations, systems, and group level team. You will be the Human Resources Business Partner for the Group (currently a growing team of over 40 people located in 8 countries), working closely with Jaza’s Talent Lead, HR Lead, and Learning and Development Lead to nurture our talent, drive performance, coach managers, and build our culture.
	This is a foundational role in a lean team. You are a 'team of one' for our Group employees.
	The ideal candidate thrives in ambiguity, loves building HR infrastructure from scratch, and shifts seamlessly from high-level leadership coaching to executing monthly data changes and payroll data verification.
	This position involves strategic partnership with global leadership, hands-on oversight of employee relations, and compliance across multiple jurisdictions.


Roles And Responsibilities
Business Partner:


	Act as the dedicated HR Business Partner for the Group (global) team of 40+ employees dispersed across 8 countries.
	Coach and advise Group managers on performance management, employee development, disciplinary actions, and organizational design.
	Coach leaders on management skills that scale across distributed, cross-cultural teams, ensuring a consistent Jaza leadership style.
	Lead employee relations for the Group team, including managing complex issues, grievances, investigations, and conflict resolution.
	Partner directly with the Talent Lead on Group talent acquisition efforts, ensuring a seamless candidate experience and strong onboarding outcomes.
	Collaborate with the Learning and Development Lead to ensure Group team training needs are identified and addressed.
	Manage the full annual performance management cycle for the Group team, including goal setting, reviews, and evaluations.
	Act as a culture champion and drive employee wellbeing, engagement activities, and culture action planning. 


HR Administration:


	Maintain accurate and compliant employee records across all HR systems and payroll platforms, in line with internal policies and legal standards.
	Submit timely and accurate monthly group payroll data to the rewards team/finance for processing across all jurisdictions.
	Support and manage employee benefits administration globally, including documentation and processing.
	Compliance, Risk Management, and Vendor Management
	Manage group compliance and HR administrative processes.
	Own the strategic relationship with our Employer of Record (EOR) partners and leverage EORs and external counsel to build a compliant, unified global employee experience 
	Liaise directly with local labor law experts in all operating countries (including Canada, China, Tanzania, Nigeria, Kenya, Uganda, and the United States) to maintain current knowledge and ensure full legal compliance.


How We’ll Measure Success:


	Group managers report feeling highly supported and equipped to lead distributed, cross-cultural teams effectively, measured via leadership feedback loops and manager-readiness surveys. 
	Zero compliance issues across all operating countries
	High accuracy in employee data and timely payroll submission
	Positive engagement survey results and clear action planning on feedback
	100% completion rate for the global performance review cycle


Key Working Relationships:


	Head of People and Culture
	Country HR Leads
	Group and Country Hiring Managers
	Talent Lead (group)
	Learning and Development Lead (group)


Why You Should Apply:


	Be part of one of the most exciting growth phases at Jaza. We’ve proven our model, now we’re scaling fast, and your work will directly shape our teams, culture, and long-term impact, giving you a front-row seat to meaningful systems-building and rapid organizational growth.
	Work with an international team across Canada, China, Tanzania, Nigeria, Kenya, Uganda, and the United States, gaining exposure to diverse markets, leadership styles, and cross-cultural collaboration that will accelerate your professional growth.


Qualifications / Experience & Competencies


	Bachelor’s Degree in Human Resource Management, Law, Business Administration, Psychology, or a related field.
	5 to 8 years of agile HR experience, with at least 3 years actively acting as an HR Business Partner or People Partner supporting teams across multiple African countries.
	Expertise in managing employee relations and deep, practical understanding of labor laws and practices across multiple African countries (required); experience in Asian/Western markets (Canada, China, US) is a strong plus.
	Comfortable managing relationships with Employer of Record (EOR) companies and external legal counsel.
	Detail-oriented and organized, with a strong ability to manage confidentiality and sensitive information.
	Strong communication, relationship-building, and conflict resolution skills, with a high degree of cross-cultural fluency and professionalism.
	Self-motivated and adaptable, comfortable working in a fast-paced, startup environment.
